channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Rally attendee steals the show by mimicking Trump’s gestures
item.description: A rally attendee seated directly behind Trump drew attention by mimicking the president.

item.title: France wildfires force evacuation of over 20,000 people from tourist town
item.description: About 800 firefighters and hundreds of gendarmes and soldiers deployed to battle the flames west of Bordeaux.

item.title: Videos show the moment police officer shoots man on street in Wisconsin
item.description: Outrage is growing after videos show a police officer fatally shooting a man in the US state of Wisconsin.

item.title: White House says will not block Tate brothers’ extradition to UK
item.description: Social media influencers to be extradited after UK authorities file dozens of new sex crime charges against them.

item.title: Deadly monsoon floods kill at least 40 in India
item.description: At least 40 people have died and up to 80 remain missing after heavy monsoon rains triggered severe flooding.

item.title: US government cancels subpoena against three New York Times reporters
item.description: Withdrawal is latest instance of government's efforts to crack down on critical media facing setbacks in court.

item.title: How Sudan war is putting cancer patients at risk
item.description: How Sudan war is putting cancer patients at risk

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike over exam paper leaks
item.description: ⁠Sonam ⁠Wangchuk says he ended his fast to demand education minister's resignation 'in view of possible violence'.

item.title: Minnesota man who killed Democratic lawmaker sentenced to life in US prison
item.description: Judge says that long sentence is merited for slaying of state Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman and her husband Mark.

item.title: Journalist killed in Mexico after years of exposing corruption
item.description: Mexican journalist Alejandro Leyva Aguilar was shot dead while eating at a street food stall in Oaxaca.

item.title: Resumption of oil exports: Will Yemen recover its economic lifeline?
item.description: Yemen has proven oil reserves estimated at three billion barrels, but the security environment hinders its export.

item.title: Can India’s youth-led protest movement bring lasting change?
item.description: Protesters refuse to back down until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.

item.title: Children wounded in Israeli strike on Khan Younis
item.description: Children were rushed to hospital after an Israeli strike hit a tent sheltering displaced people in Khan Younis.

item.title: World Bank estimates Venezuela earthquakes caused $19.6bn in damage
item.description: Report says 47 percent of physical damage from pair of earthquakes impacted residential buildings.

item.title: Iraqi PM makes first official visit to Iran in bid to deepen ties
item.description: Ali al-Zaidi holds talks with senior Iranian officials on bilateral cooperation, shared interests and security.

item.title: Bordeaux wildfire burns 3,100 hectares, 20,000 evacuated
item.description: A wildfire near Bordeaux, France, has burned 3,100 hectares (7,660 acres) of forest

item.title: UK’s Burnham tells Scotland new independence vote is off limits
item.description: Visiting Scotland, Burnham says a new referendum would detract from efforts to grow the economy.

item.title: What are the security implications of the US-Saudi nuclear deal?
item.description: Concerns are growing over a nuclear arms race in the Middle East.

item.title: Far-right Israeli incursion heightens tensions at Al-Aqsa ahead of election
item.description: Israeli far-right Minister Ben-Gvir joins provocative incursion at Al-Aqsa, escalating tensions ahead of elections.

item.title: Oil surges past $100 in a first since May as Middle East conflicts rage
item.description: Oil prices rise for a fifth day after Yemen's Houthis say they struck Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea.

item.title: A Game of Double Standards
item.description: Qatar 2022: a World Cup that exposed football’s politics, power struggles, and double standards.

item.title: Trump: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on Saudi joining Abraham Accords
item.description: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: President Aoun visits White House to discuss Hezbollah
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khodr brings you the latest from Beirut.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: President Aoun visits White House to discuss Hezbollah
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khodr brings you the latest from Beirut after Lebanon’s President meets with Donald Trump.

item.title: Yemen faces a new challenge amid prospects of confrontation
item.description: What is the military balance of power in Yemen and the likelihood of confrontation between the government and Houthis?
